text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY CLINIC
PRE-OPERATIVE ASSESSMENT NOTE

PATIENT: Denise Okafor
ATTENDING: [Attending Oncologist]
VISIT TYPE: Established patient, pre-surgical evaluation

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT
Ms. Okafor is a 49-year-old West African-descent woman with Stage IIB (T2N1M0) triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant carrier, presenting today for pre-operative oncology assessment following completion of neoadjuvant chemotherapy and immunotherapy. She has completed all planned neoadjuvant treatment (ddAC x4 cycles + Keytruda, then weekly Taxol x12 weeks + pembro) and is now being evaluated prior to definitive surgery. Post-neoadjuvant MRI has been reviewed. Surgical options have been discussed with the breast surgery and plastic surgery teams and today's visit focuses on finalizing the oncologic plan, addressing patient questions, and ensuring fitness for surgery.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Okafor was diagnosed with left breast TNBC approximately 5 months ago following presentation with a self-palpated left upper outer quadrant mass. Biopsy confirmed grade 3 infiltrating ductal carcinoma, ER 0%, PR 0%, HER2 1+ (non-amplified by FISH), Ki-67 85%, with 1 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes involved on initial biopsy. NGS demonstrated a germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT), somatic TP53 p.R248W, PIK3CA p.H1047R, and HRD-positive status (HRD score 68). PD-L1 CPS 18 by 22C3 assay. TILs 35% stromal.

She was enrolled on the KEYNOTE-522 neoadjuvant regimen: pembro 200 mg IV Q3W x4 cycles with concurrent dose-dense doxo/cyclo Q2W (ddAC), followed by weekly Taxol 80 mg/m2 x12 weeks combined with pembro Q3W. Notable treatment course events:
- Keytruda Cycle 2 held for grade 2 immune-related thyroiditis (thyrotoxic phase transitioning to hypothyroid phase); patient now stably on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily.
- PTX dose reduced to 64 mg/m2 (80%) from week 7 onward for grade 2 sensory peripheral neuropathy; duloxetine 30 mg daily started for symptom management.
- All 4 ddAC cycles and all 12 planned weekly Taxol doses were completed.

Post-neoadjuvant breast MRI was performed and reviewed in detail at today's visit. Imaging shows a residual enhancing mass in the left breast upper outer quadrant measuring approximately 1.6 x 1.4 cm (down from 3.2 x 2.8 cm at baseline), representing a meaningful reduction but with clearly persistent residual disease. Residual left axillary node with cortical thickening persists at 6 mm. Radiographic impression is consistent with a partial response; no pathologic complete response is expected. No new or distant lesions identified.

Given the imaging findings -- residual mass with persistent nodal involvement -- and in the context of her germline BRCA2 status, surgical options were extensively discussed across multiple visits with the breast surgical oncology and plastic surgery teams. Ms. Okafor had a detailed conversation about breast-conserving surgery (lumpectomy) with re-excision as needed, axillary lymph node dissection (ALND), versus left modified radical mastectomy. She also expressed strong interest in contralateral prophylactic mastectomy given her BRCA2 carrier status. After considering reconstruction options, she has elected to proceed with left modified radical mastectomy (levels I-II ALND) with immediate tissue expander placement, and right prophylactic mastectomy with immediate tissue expander reconstruction. This decision was made thoughtfully over multiple discussions and is consistent with her goals and risk-reduction priorities.

Today she reports feeling well overall. She is fatigued but functional. Neuropathy in hands and feet is improved since Taxol completion -- currently grade 1 at most, with mild persistent tingling bilaterally in fingertips and toes that does not limit her activities. She is working part-time (she has taken a temporary reduced load from her teaching position). No new symptoms. No dyspnea, chest pain, or bone pain. No abdominal symptoms. Bowel and bladder function normal.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: Mild residual fatigue, improving over the past 2-3 weeks since PTX completion. No fever, chills, or night sweats.
Neurologic: Persistent mild (grade 1) bilateral distal sensory neuropathy, improving. No motor deficits.
Endocrine: No palpitations, heat/cold intolerance, or tremor since initiation of levothyroxine. Last TFTs reviewed today (see below).
Cardiovascular: No dyspnea on exertion, no lower extremity edema, no palpitations.
Pulmonary: No cough, no dyspnea.
Gastrointestinal: No nausea/vomiting at this point. No diarrhea, constipation, or mucositis. Appetite improving.
Musculoskeletal: Arthralgia mild and improved since PTX completed. No focal bone pain.
Dermatologic: No rash. No palmar/plantar changes.
Psychiatric: Anxiety present but manageable; patient connected with social work. Coping well overall.
All other systems reviewed and negative.

---

MEDICATIONS (current)
- Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO daily (immune-related hypothyroidism on Keytruda)
- Duloxetine 30 mg PO daily (chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy)
- Ondansetron 4 mg PO Q8H PRN nausea (rarely using now)
- Multivitamin daily

ALLERGIES: NKDA

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital signs: BP 118/72 mmHg, HR 68 bpm (regular), RR 16, Temp 36.8°C, SpO2 98% on room air, Weight 75.2 kg, BSA 1.73 m2.
ECOG performance status: 0.

General: Pleasant, well-appearing woman in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Conversational, appropriately anxious about upcoming surgery.

Head/Neck: Anicteric sclerae. No thyromegaly on palpation. No supraclavicular or cervical lymphadenopathy appreciated.

Breast exam:
- Left breast: Firm, irregular mass palpable in upper outer quadrant, approximately 2 cm on palpation, consistent with residual tumor (imaging 1.6 cm; exam somewhat limited by density). No skin dimpling, no skin ulceration. No nipple discharge. Overlying skin intact.
- Left axilla: Single firm, slightly enlarged lymph node palpable in left axilla, approximately 1 cm. Non-tender.
- Right breast: No dominant mass, no axillary adenopathy. No skin changes.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1/S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No JVD.

Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheeze or crackles.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly. No palpable masses.

Extremities: No edema. No calf tenderness.

Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 5/5 throughout. Sensation: slightly diminished light touch at distal toes and fingertips bilaterally, symmetric, non-progressive. Gait normal.

Skin: No rash. No palmar erythema. No jaundice.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (reviewed today)
CBC: WBC 5.8 x10^9/L (diff: neutrophils 62%, lymphocytes 26%), Hgb 11.6 g/dL (slightly low, stable from recovery post-chemotherapy), Plt 224 x10^9/L. No neutropenia, no thrombocytopenia. Anemia mild, acceptable for surgery per anesthesia.

Comprehensive metabolic panel: Na 139, K 4.1, Cr 0.82 mg/dL, BUN 14. ALT 22 U/L, AST 19 U/L, Alk Phos 68 U/L, Total bili 0.6 mg/dL. LFTs normal -- no ongoing hepatic toxicity from prior therapy.

Thyroid function: TSH 1.8 mIU/L (normal on current levothyroxine dose). Free T4 1.1 ng/dL (normal). Thyroid function stable -- no dose adjustment needed.

Coagulation: PT/INR 1.0, aPTT 28 sec. Normal.

Cardiac: ECHO performed prior to neoadjuvant initiation showed LVEF 62%; no repeat echo ordered given no new cardiac symptoms and cumulative anthracycline dose not exceeding threshold.

---

IMAGING REVIEWED
Post-neoadjuvant breast MRI: Residual enhancing lesion left breast UOQ, approximately 1.6 x 1.4 cm (down from 3.2 x 2.8 cm pre-treatment). Single residual left axillary node with persistent cortical thickening, 6 mm. No satellite lesions. No skin involvement on MRI. No contralateral findings. No evidence of distant disease on staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed 6 weeks prior (last staging imaging prior to end of Taxol phase showed no distant metastatic disease).

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IIB TNBC, left breast, post-neoadjuvant treatment, with partial radiographic response (residual disease on MRI, non-pCR expected):
   - Patient has completed the full planned neoadjuvant KEYNOTE-522 regimen (ddAC x4 + Keytruda; weekly pacli x12 + Keytruda).
   - Post-neoadjuvant MRI demonstrates residual disease (1.6 cm mass, residual axillary node). This is concordant with partial response and we do not anticipate pCR on final pathology. The implications of non-pCR in TNBC are discussed -- specifically the importance of adjuvant escalation with Xeloda and the discussion of Lynparza given her BRCA2 germline status -- but these decisions will be finalized after final surgical pathology is available and RCB can be fully calculated.
   - Surgical plan confirmed: Left modified radical mastectomy (MRM) with ipsilateral level I-II axillary lymph node dissection + right prophylactic mastectomy with bilateral immediate tissue expander reconstruction. Patient has been thoroughly counseled regarding this decision. She understands the rationale for full ALND given persistent nodal imaging (re-sentinel node biopsy alone would be suboptimal in this setting given prior involvement and persistent abnormal node on imaging). Breast surgery and plastic surgery teams have been coordinated and the case has been scheduled.
   - BRCA2 germline carrier status: Strongly supports consideration of prophylactic contralateral mastectomy, which patient has elected. This has been discussed in the context of her lifetime risk reduction goals. Genetic counseling has been completed; patient is enrolled in the BRCA2 registry.
   - Post-mastectomy radiation therapy (PMRT): Will be discussed following surgery and final pathology. Given known nodal involvement at diagnosis (1 positive sentinel node at biopsy) and likely residual nodal disease post-NAC, PMRT to left chest wall and regional nodes is anticipated. Radiation oncology has been cc'd on this case; formal consultation to be obtained post-operatively.
   - Adjuvant systemic therapy: Pending final pathology. We have discussed the CREATE-X/OlympiA data with the patient in the context of her non-pCR. Xeloda adjuvant therapy will likely be recommended. ola will also be discussed given germline BRCA2 status and anticipated high-risk residual disease; sequencing/timing of these agents will be determined post-operatively.

2. Immune-related hypothyroidism (sequela of pembro):
   - Stable on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily. TSH 1.8 mIU/L today -- no change needed.
   - Continue levothyroxine through surgical period; anesthesia team notified to continue perioperatively.

3. Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy (grade 1, improving):
   - Residual mild distal sensory neuropathy after Taxol. Grade 1, not functionally limiting.
   - Duloxetine 30 mg daily continued.
   - Not a contraindication to surgery. Will continue to monitor.

4. Pre-operative clearance:
   - Anemia (Hgb 11.6 g/dL): Mild, likely chemotherapy-related recovery. Acceptable for elective surgery per pre-op anesthesia assessment. Discussed with surgical team.
   - No coagulopathy. LFTs normal. Renal function normal.
   - ECOG PS 0 -- fit for surgery.
   - Cardiac status adequate; no repeat echo indicated at this time.
   - Patient is medically cleared from oncology standpoint for planned bilateral mastectomy with reconstruction.

5. BRCA2 counseling / risk-reducing salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO):
   - Long-term RRSO will be discussed with gynecologic oncology at appropriate time post-recovery from breast surgery. Given that she is 49 and premenopausal, timing of RRSO is an important consideration; this is deferred until after the immediate surgical and adjuvant therapy period.

6. Follow-up:
   - Breast surgery team will coordinate surgical scheduling (next 1-2 weeks anticipated).
   - Post-operative oncology visit planned at approximately 3-4 weeks post-surgery to review final pathology, discuss adjuvant plan, and ensure wound healing.
   - Patient instructed to contact the clinic for any new symptoms, fever, bleeding concerns.

Time spent in counseling and coordination: approximately 45 minutes.

[Electronically signed by Attending Medical Oncologist]
